name: Ladder Climb
description: A constrained vertical traversal state that attaches the character to a ladder, maps input to rung movement, and handles safe entry and exit points.
design_notes: Ladder climb is simple only if attach and detach rules are explicit. Define ladder_id, top and bottom attach points, rung indexing, input axis ownership, collision mask changes, animation sync, blocked exits, and how the camera behaves when the player is constrained to a vertical path.
design_purpose: Provide reliable vertical traversal in narrow or authored spaces.
Give level designers a constrained climb route with clear entry and exit points.
Bridge ground movement, ledge traversal, and platforming without requiring jump precision.
player_fantasy: The player feels attached to a clear climb path, safely moving up or down while still choosing when to exit.

parameters.name: ladder_axis_velocity
parameters.description: Movement velocity along the ladder axis while attached.

parameters.name: ladder_attach_margin_units
parameters.description: Distance from ladder volume accepted for attachment.

parameters.name: ladder_rung_spacing_units
parameters.description: Spacing used for animation foot and hand alignment.

parameters.name: ladder_exit_probe_height_units
parameters.description: Vertical clearance checked before leaving at top or bottom.

parameters.name: ladder_detach_buffer_ms
parameters.description: Buffered jump, drop, or exit input while attached.

parameters.name: ladder_camera_profile_id
parameters.description: Camera behavior while the character is constrained to ladder movement.

edge_cases: The top exit can be blocked after the player starts climbing, requiring a hold, drop, or retry state.
Two players or AI agents can attach to the same ladder segment in multiplayer or crowd scenes.
Jumping off a ladder needs a clear impulse vector and restored collision layers.
Moving ladders need attach transforms relative to ladder_id rather than world-only positions.
common_bugs: The character remains in no-gravity mode after detaching from ladder_id.
Input axis mapping flips when camera direction crosses behind the ladder.
Exit placement ignores capsule clearance and clips the player into the floor above.
Animation rung alignment drifts because rung index is derived from render position instead of climb distance.
balancing_notes: Fast ladders reduce frustration but make vertical exposure and combat risk less meaningful.
Auto-attach is useful in tight spaces but can steal control near ledges or interactables.
Ladder exits should be readable enough that players know whether to climb, jump, or drop.
accessibility_notes: Allow simple up/down input and avoid requiring held diagonal inputs to stay attached.
Use distinct visual and audio cues for valid exit points, not only camera angle or controller vibration.

implementation_notes.unity: Use trigger volumes tagged with ladder_id and explicit attach points. On attach, switch movement_state_id to ladder, disable ordinary ground acceleration, project input onto the ladder axis, and run capsule clearance before top or bottom exit.
implementation_notes.godot: Model ladders as Area nodes with exported attach transforms. While attached, update character position along the ladder axis in _physics_process and restore collision masks through one detach function.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use a ladder interaction component or Smart Object that grants a custom movement mode. Replicate ladder_id and normalized rung position so clients and server agree on exit checks and animation sync.
implementation_notes.web: Store ladder_state with ladder id, normalized position, rung index, and pending detach input. Resolve axis movement in fixed ticks and re-enable normal collision only after exit validation succeeds.

example_games.title: Spelunky
example_games.note: Uses ladders as clear vertical routes in platforming spaces.

example_games.title: Half-Life 2
example_games.note: Uses ladders for constrained vertical traversal in first-person levels.
